content.full_text:
FPP

3238 E. Villa Knolls Dr.
Pasadena, CA 91107
May 23, 1990

May 29 11:19 AM '90

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Thank you for sending me your 1990 Information Manual. In reading it over,
however, I need to have a point clarified.

The Manual C for General Purpose Recipient Committees (Political Committees and
Broad Based Political Committees) which you sent me has caused me to wonder if the
California Federation of Republican Women is classified under the new contribution
limits (page 13) as a "Person" or a "Political Committee"?

According to the definitions we should be a "Person":
1. Our objectives are to promote an informed public through political
education and activity, to increase the effectiveness of the
cause of good government, to promote a wider knowledge of the principles
and policies of the Republican Party and to encourage active citizenship, etc.
2. Funds are NOT raised to make political donations but to pay our per capita
dues to the other levels of the Federation, pay the expenses of program
speakers and bulletins, and the expenses of sending delegates to other
Federation functions such as conventions and seminars. Some Clubs do make
political donations if their treasuery permits it, but the Federation does
not encourage it.
3. We are an independent organization and not connected in any way to the
official Republican Party organizations.
4. In the past we have followed the same general guidelines as a labor
organization since our members pay dues which some portion might be used
as a political donation, and a labor organization is listed on page 7
in the Manual under Person for the purposes of the contribution limitations.

I would appreciate your response as soon as possible. Thank you

(Mrs. H. Alfred Dunn)
California Federation of Republican Women

[The image appears to be a heavily faded or degraded photocopy of a typed document. Much of the text is illegible due to the poor quality, fading, and multiple layers of copying. While there appear to be several paragraphs of text visible, the individual words and sentences cannot be reliably read or accurately transcribed without significant guesswork. The document appears to be several pages long based on the layout, but the specific content cannot be determined with confidence from this image quality.]

In reading it over however, I need to have a point clarified. The Manual C for General Purpose Recipient Committees (Political Committees and Broad Based Political Committees) which you sent me has caused me to wonder if the California Federation of Republican Women is classified under the new contribution limits (page 13) as a "Person" or a "Political Committee"? According to the definitions we should be a "Person": 1. Our objectives are to promote an informed public through political education and activity, to increase the effectiveness of women in the cause of good government, to promote a wider knowledge of the principles and policies of the Republican Party and to encourage active citizenship, etc. 2. Funds are NOT raised to make political donations but to pay our per capita dues to the other levels of the Federation, pay the expenses of program speakers and bulletins, and the expenses of sending delegates to other Federation functions such as conventions and seminars. Some Clubs do make political donations if their treasurey permits it, but the Federation does not encourage it. 3. We are an independent organization and not connected in any way to the official Republican Party organizations. 4. In the past we have followed the same general guidelines as a labor organization since our members pay dues which some portion might be used as a political donation, and a labor organization is listed on page 7 in the Manual under Person for the purposes of the contribution limitations. I would appreciate your response as soon as possible. Thank you (Mrs. H.
